babyDevelopment: The baby is now practicing 'breathing' movements. The brain is continuing to develop rapidly, with more complex nerve connections forming. The baby's sleep patterns are becoming more regular. This week marks the end of the second trimester.
bodyChanges: Welcome to the final stage of your second trimester! You might feel more pressure in your pelvic area as the baby grows. Some women experience 'leg cramps' and 'hemorrhoids' due to increased pressure and hormones.
tips: Stay hydrated and maintain a high-fiber diet to prevent constipation
Perform gentle calf stretches to manage leg cramps
Review your birth plan and nursery preparation

do: Incorporate more calcium for baby's bone growth
Practice relaxation techniques for better sleep
Elevate your feet to reduce swelling
dont: Eat large, heavy meals before bedtime
Ignore persistent itching on the abdomen

travelAdvisory.note: Still a good time for travel; prioritize comfort and hydration.

warningSignsToWatch: Sudden, extreme swelling in face or hands
milestoneDescription: You're entering the home stretch as the second trimester concludes.
